title: EoTV and EoE Instrumentality split Was Kaworu really Adam
display_title: EoTV and EoE Instrumentality split Was Kaworu really Adam
wikitext:
[]: EoTV and EoE Instrumentality (split: Was Kaworu really Adam)             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Thu Jan 08, 2009 9:33 am NemZ wrote: ... and in EOTV I read it as him (and everyone else with the possible exception of Rei) being just a fragment of Shinji's own mind, not the real Kaji.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Jan 08, 2009 12:25 pm EOTV, at least from my view, has a structure of: 1) Main characters in states of isolated self-reflection 2) Shinji interacting with a few of them 3) Shinji retreating in on himself with this new information 4) Shinji comes to terms with himself and the world Kaji appears in part 2 as a figment of Misato's mind while Shinji observes, both in the form of memories and as a choir of her own projected insecurities and how she believes those individuals would react if they knew the truth of her carnal desires and self-destructive impulses. Kaji also appears occasionally in parts 3 and 4, but by that time Shinji is either alone in his splintering thoughts or accompanied by Rei. Here he once again is part of a chorus of accusers, but later takes on a voice of confident assertions... a projection of Shinji's more courageous aspects in contrast to more self-deprecating voices or simply encouraging ones. He plays a role parallel to Gendo, who I also don't believe is actually a part of instrumentality. EOE is similar, save that part 1 happens in the real world and part 2 is in pre-impact instrumentality, but -does- appear to be a simultaneous sharing... but only among Shinji, Rei, and the recent dead, with Kaji nowhere to be found save an out-of-focus memory from Misato. In both cases other such mutual sharings certainly may be happening (Asuka/Misato would probably be pretty damned interesting...), but we don't see them. All we get is what Shinji partakes in. (and yes, split would be welcome)

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Jan 08, 2009 2:05 pm Shin-seiki wrote: Pro-tip: just about everything Instrumentality-related, in both EoTV and EoE, makes vastly more sense if you just accept that the lines spoken by any given character are originating from the mind of said character. 'It's-all-in-Shinji's-mind-LOL' makes the whole thing nonsensical.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Jan 08, 2009 2:15 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: (Although it's beyond me WHY Pen Pen was in Instrumentality since although he's a Lilin, he's not a human...)

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Jan 08, 2009 4:35 pm In the "In the Case of X" incidents we clearly see characters interacting; why, just for lack of time -- as indicated here The thing that people lost, in other words, the complementation of the mind has begun. However, there is not enough time to describe the entire process. Therefore, we will examine the complementation of the mind of a single boy named Ikari Shinji.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Jan 08, 2009 4:52 pm I picture EOTV's version of instrumentality as much more personal... individuals working out their own issues while others get to watch. It isn't interactive so much as mutually voyeuristic, and though many people may be watching the same person's mindscape they will have no interaction, no 'peanut gallery' moments... well, at least not at these initial stages. If the instrumentality process were allowed to fully progress that would be a likely later step in the mental co-evolutionary process. The various characters who appear to offer informed rebuttal to the individual's denials are projections of the self, providing the expected feedback the individual believes would result from such revelations. To put it another way, it's kind of like the Star Trek TNG episode in which the ship gains sentience and creates a sort of subconscious processing center on the holodeck, with various characters representing individual facets of the whole. All the various players are equivalent, but our viewpoint isn't. All we get to see are the major characters on their own terms, themselves analyzed while Shinji watches, and then his crucible process in detail. Technically everyone should have these moments should also be permitted to view the trials of others, or at least those whom they are close to. It's worth noting Shinji doesn't peer into the minds of Kensuke, Toji, or even his father. Incidentally, I think EOTV may have been a variation in which Rei did not betray Gendo at the crucial moment. ...I also don't know for sure if non-humans are really involved, nor believe that the long dead have any stake in the matter.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Jan 08, 2009 5:18 pm Reichu: Kaji was dead for awhile before Instrumentality began and yet he was a part of it. That's what I meant. NemZ wrote: Incidentally, I think EOTV may have been a variation in which Rei did not betray Gendo at the crucial moment.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Thu Jan 08, 2009 7:08 pm #25 wrote: TEXT: In the Case of Misato Katsuragi Shinji: This is the me that exists in your mind, Misato. Misato: And at the same time, the me that exists in your mind, right?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Jan 08, 2009 10:39 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: Reichu: Kaji was dead for awhile before Instrumentality began and yet he was a part of it. That's what I meant.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Jan 09, 2009 11:06 am Put it this way... I don't think anyone who died before 3I was involved unless Rei specifically quantum magic-ed her way back in time to collect them at the moment of their death. I also don't think Kaji was chosen in this manner.

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Fri Jan 09, 2009 11:29 am NemZ wrote: Put it this way... I don't think anyone who died before 3I was involved unless Rei specifically quantum magic-ed her way back in time to collect them at the moment of their death. I also don't think Kaji was chosen in this manner.

[]:             Quote Post by VoidEater » Fri Jan 09, 2009 5:25 pm I would say that whenever someone identifies themself as "the ___ in your mind", HIP is (at least) incomplete. When attained, HIP renders one mind, without the opportunity for different versions of people ("mental models") in individual minds. I believe this is all meta-message: how we have a mental model of other people in our minds, and how we often interact with those models rather than with the actual person "out there". Perhaps the start of HIP is blurring the boundaries, making the distinction between mental image and other person more clear; it seems to me, all the "I am the ___ in your mind" is Shinji's decision making process in deciding whether to go forward with HIP. He is engaging with his memories, Kaji included. We are not seeing HIP in these scenes. Now I'll go back and watch it again and see if I believe that...

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Fri Jan 09, 2009 7:15 pm NemZ wrote: ... and in EOTV I read it as him (and everyone else with the possible exception of Rei) being just a fragment of Shinji's own mind, not the real Kaji.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Jan 09, 2009 7:51 pm thewayneiac wrote: My guess is that we are supposed to assume a practical time limit (that includes Kaji).

[]:             Quote Post by MatrixRefugee » Fri Jan 09, 2009 7:55 pm Reichu wrote: Sailor Star Dust wrote: Reichu: Kaji was dead for awhile before Instrumentality began and yet he was a part of it. That's what I meant.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Jan 09, 2009 8:20 pm thewayneiac wrote: If Kaji only appeared only in Misato flashbacks, I'd say that maybe Shinji was just seeing her memories, but he's there at the end of the episode talking about rainy days being fun too, along with everyone else.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Jan 09, 2009 8:44 pm NemZ wrote: Put it this way... I don't think anyone who died before 3I was involved unless Rei specifically quantum magic-ed her way back in time to collect them at the moment of their death.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Fri Jan 09, 2009 9:19 pm There are plenty of instances where the only way the dialog and circumstances make sense are to assume that the speakers are interacting with each other. Asuka: You just want people to need you! Shinji: You might be right. Asuka: All you do is wait, wanting people to give you happiness! False happiness! Rei: But that goes for you too.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Jan 10, 2009 8:55 am Okay, I thought I had found a way to make some sense out of things but now I flat-out admit that at this point I don't have a clue how to interpret the last two episodes beyond just the general "get your shit together, Shinji" theme. The cases don't follow any specific limitations or always have clear transitions. What is physically real is mixed up with introspective projections, actual mental contacts, and outright fabrications. There is no way to concretely tell who is really participating and who is just a projection or memory from scene to scene, further complicated by recycled dialog and scenes. Damn it Anno! Yeah, I'm starting to think I should just ignore EOTV from now on, just like the original versions of 21-24.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Jan 10, 2009 11:55 am But EoTV helps make clear what EOE doesn't (actually stating Instrumentality's occuring for one thing.) You really can't have one ending without the other.
